parsed_review.summary: CP00-470 is a Risk-A, 10-unit descriptor-only closeout pack covering RP15.P07.M03.S11-S20 (Primary Implementation Slice). Verified end-to-end consistency across registry.js (CP470 pack binding, requirements, no-write attestation), service.js (descriptor/case-set generators), validators.js (coverage + slice validation), the analytics contract, the live closeout-pack plan, next-pack-queue, README, model.test.js, and the RP15 validator script. Pack binding, range, upstream/next pack, next_subphase S21, deliverable distribution {failure_recovery:3, implementation:1, hermes_evidence:2, fixture:1, test:2, security_audit:1}=10, and micro_title distribution {Primary Implementation Slice:10} all match with deviation_from_plan:false confirmed against the actual plan block. Every generated row is descriptor-only (descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false); freezeCp470Result asserts no runtime dispatch, no real-data load, no state/audit writes, no Hermes runtime receipt, and no leaks. A targeted scan found no fs/child_process/fetch/eval/process.env usage in packages/analytics/src, confirming pure descriptor code. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false (enforced by validators and tests). Hermes appears only as a deterministic evidence-packet/gate (H15) descriptor with emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, not product runtime. The pack does not overclaim: production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false with a descriptor-verified flag. Review-hardening preflight passed with valid parseable JSON (no auth failure, no 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call output); tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob; no invalid review attempts; unrelated dirty files correctly excluded from pack scope. One informational finding: the post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) and post-review gated validations (closeout-pack:validate, git diff --check --cached) are by-design not yet present and must be generated and pass before commit. No P0/P1/P2 issues; Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not the final approval authority.

findings.title: Post-review artifacts and gated validations pending by design
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts and evidence_refs list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-470/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in that directory. command-evidence.json (notes) also defers npm run closeout-pack:validate, the review run/normalize/receipt validations, and git diff --check --cached to post-review. This is consistent with the documented hardening_sequence (read_only_claude_run -> normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation -> commit): these artifacts are produced AFTER this read-only review step, so their absence now is expected and is not a defect in the pack implementation. It is flagged only so the human adjudicator confirms the gate is satisfied before closeout is marked complete.
findings.recommendation: Before committing CP00-470, generate the normalized review result, receipt, adjudication, and construction-inspection artifacts from this accepted review, run the post-review gated validations (npm run closeout-pack:validate and git diff --check --cached), and confirm exactly one valid pack review receipt is recorded in pack_level_claude_review.valid_review_receipts.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected by design; not blocking. To be satisfied by the post-review steps of the hardening sequence prior to commit.
